name: g15_transport_shell_grammar
purpose: Anti-drift anchor for the G15 transport shell page and related renderings.

ontology.description: The transport shell is interpreted as a sheeted system with an upstairs, a downstairs, and stairs in between.

shell.description: Vertices are anchored at the 8 cube corners, the 6 cube-face centroids, and the cube centroid.

walk.interpretation: The colored traces represent the walk through 3 sectors.
walk.description: The walk traverses the shell by moving across sector sheets with two lifts and three descents.
walk.stairs_rule: In the cube lens, the walk is read as starting and stopping on the stairs.

register_bridge.note: This shell grammar bridges the existing register/frontier grammar into the transport-shell rendering.

sheet_lens.name: Sheet lens
sheet_lens.purpose: Analysis view with tilted sheets preserved.

cube_lens.name: Cube lens
cube_lens.purpose: Flattened teaching view with cube-first reading.
cube_lens.human_note: In cube lens, v0 is the distinguished orienting anchor: the stairs midpoint, which coincides with the cube centroid. The walk is read as starting and stopping on the stairs. This is a visual shell assignment, not a renumbering of the theorem object.

tetra_lens.name: Tetra lens
tetra_lens.purpose: Simplex-first teaching view with 4 corners, 6 edge midpoints, 4 face centroids, and 1 centroid.
tetra_lens.human_note: In tetra lens, v0 is the tetra centroid. This is a visual shell assignment only, not a renumbering of the theorem object.

anti_drift_rules: Do not reinterpret the shell as a generic graph-only diagram.
Do not demote upstairs/downstairs/stairs to incidental styling.
Do not remove the 15-anchor shell ontology.
Do not treat the colored traces as arbitrary decoration.
Do not treat lens anchor assignments as theorem-object renumberings.
In cube lens, preserve v0 as the distinguished stairs midpoint / cube centroid anchor.
Do not treat tetra-lens anchor assignments as theorem-object renumberings.
